title: Routing Architecture

options: The server sends a new HTML page for every link
The browser updates the URL and changes the UI without a full page reload
questions.explanation: This makes the app feel fast and 'native' compared to traditional websites.

questions.question_text: Which library is the industry standard for routing in React?
questions.explanation: React Router is the most widely used library for web-based React apps.

options: Checks if the user has a browser
Provides the context for routing and syncs the UI with the URL
questions.explanation: It should wrap your entire application.

questions.question_text: Which component is used to define a mapping between a URL path and a Component?
What is the difference between <Link> and regular <a> tags in React Router?
options: <Link> prevents a full page reload; <a> causes the browser to refresh from the server
questions.explanation: Always use <Link> (or <NavLink>) to maintain the 'Single Page' experience.

options: You can't do this in React
questions.explanation: The colon ':' indicates a variable part of the URL.

questions.question_text: Which Hook is used to access URL parameters (like 'id') in a component?
questions.explanation: useParams returns an object of the dynamic segments of the current URL.

options: Defining routes inside other routes to share a common layout
questions.explanation: It allows specialized UIs like sidebars or tabs that stay visible while the main content changes.

questions.question_text: What does the <Outlet /> component do in nested routing?
options: It’s a place where you buy components
It's a placeholder where the child route's component will be rendered
questions.explanation: Think of it as a 'hole' in the parent layout that sub-pages fill.

questions.question_text: How do you programmatically navigate to another page (e.g., after a login form)?

options: A route that checks if a user is authenticated before allowing access to its component
A route that only works on HTTPS
questions.explanation: You typically wrap these in an authentication check component.

questions.question_text: Which hook provides information about the current URL (hash, search params, etc.)?
questions.explanation: useLocation returns an object representing the current location.

questions.question_text: How do you handle 404 (Not Found) pages in React Router?
options: Create a Route with path='*' at the bottom of your routes list
questions.explanation: The asterisk matches any URL that hasn't been matched by previous routes.

options: Downloading the code for a route ONLY when the user navigates to it
questions.explanation: This improves initial load time by splitting the bundle (React.lazy and Suspense).

options: Searches for variables in the code
Allows reading and modifying the query string (e.g., ?query=search)
questions.explanation: It returns an object similar to useState for handling URL query parameters.
